action.reasoning: Observation: The training script continues to crash with exit code 139 (segmentation fault). I've identified the main issues: 1) There's a mismatch between the class name in model.py (SimpleSpatioTemporalGCN) and what train.py is trying to import (SpatioTemporalGCN). Actually, looking at the current files, both model.py and train.py are using 'SpatioTemporalGCN' class name, so that's consistent. 2) The model architecture is overly complex with incorrect reshaping operations. 3) The forward method has logical errors in how it processes the data. The current model is still quite complex and might be causing memory issues. I need to simplify the model significantly to avoid these crashes. I'll create a much simpler model that correctly handles the spatiotemporal data.
